description:
You are a data-science assistant helping a researcher keep two dataset folders in sync.

Folder layout that already exists when you start the session
1. Local “authoritative” copy  
   /home/user/workspace/datasets  
      ├── sample1.csv  
      ├── sample2.csv    ← **this file is currently missing on the remote side**  
      └── sample3.csv  

2. Simulated “remote server” copy  
   /home/user/remote_share/datasets  
      ├── sample1.csv  
      └── sample3.csv  

Goal  
• Make /home/user/remote_share/datasets an exact mirror of /home/user/workspace/datasets, but do it with only 2-3 terminal commands (e.g., a single rsync invocation plus one-liner verification command).  
• Preserve timestamps of files that are already identical (so avoid blindly overwriting unchanged items).  
• Do NOT delete anything from the local directory.  
• After the sync, create a plain-text listing file called /home/user/dataset_sync.log that shows what now resides in the remote folder.

Required verification file  
/home/user/dataset_sync.log must exist and contain **exactly** the following three lines in this order, terminated by Unix newlines (LF):

sample1.csv  
sample2.csv  
sample3.csv  

No additional spaces, paths, headers, or blank lines are allowed. Automated tests will read this file verbatim, so be precise.

Hints (do NOT reveal explicit commands)  
• Any standard file-transfer tool that can copy only the missing/changed items in archive mode is acceptable.  
• Immediately after synchronizing, generate the verification list with a single command that writes to /home/user/dataset_sync.log.  
• You should need at most 2-3 commands in total to accomplish the whole task.
truth:
Initial on-disk state BEFORE the agent starts

Directories (already created, writable by user):
/home/user/workspace/datasets  
/home/user/remote_share/datasets  

Files and their contents:

/home/user/workspace/datasets/sample1.csv  
------------------------------------------------
id,value
1,10
2,20
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/workspace/datasets/sample2.csv  
------------------------------------------------
id,value
3,30
4,40
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/workspace/datasets/sample3.csv  
------------------------------------------------
id,value
5,50
6,60
------------------------------------------------

/home/user/remote_share/datasets/sample1.csv  
(identical byte-for-byte to the local copy)

/home/user/remote_share/datasets/sample3.csv  
(identical byte-for-byte to the local copy)

/home/user/remote_share/datasets/sample2.csv  
(ABSENT at start)

Expected final state AFTER correct task completion

1. /home/user/remote_share/datasets/sample2.csv now exists and is byte-for-byte identical to /home/user/workspace/datasets/sample2.csv.

2. /home/user/remote_share/datasets/sample1.csv and sample3.csv remain unchanged, with original modification timestamps.

3. /home/user/dataset_sync.log exists and contains exactly:
--------------------------------
sample1.csv
sample2.csv
sample3.csv
--------------------------------
(no trailing blank line).

Permissions: all new files should inherit default user write/read permissions; no need for executable bits.

Automated tests will:
• Verify presence and SHA-1 hashes of the three CSV files in /home/user/remote_share/datasets.  
• Check that /home/user/dataset_sync.log exists and matches the expected three-line content exactly.  
• Confirm that only 3 files (sample1.csv, sample2.csv, sample3.csv) reside in /home/user/remote_share/datasets.

No other side-effects are examined.
name: task_000000_ea8ec26d
